summary: Reviews two years of TCV tokamak advances relevant to ITER: the ITER baseline scenario, peeling-limited pedestals, high-beta_N non-inductive regimes, negative-triangularity and X-point-radiator scenarios, runaway-electron termination, and divertor exhaust, supported by gyrokinetic/fluid modelling and increasingly intertwined model-based and data-driven plasma control.

Theory of weak-to-strong generalization for reward-model learning with two-layer networks: fine-tuning under a weak, task-specialized supervisor provably elicits the strong model's relevant pre-trained feature subspace while preserving off-target features, whereas standard supervised fine-tuning catastrophically forgets correlated capabilities.

Decomposes paired SWE-bench and GPQA Diamond scores from 34 frontier models (2024-2026) into a population coupling trend plus per-release residuals: capabilities cooperate (r=+0.72), but per-lab coupling slopes vary 5x, pretraining-driven capability shifts persist, and post-training shifts reverse within a release.

Extends inference-time alignment with reference-model temperature adjustment, generalizing reward-tilted sampling to ensembles of generative reward models combined as a sharpened logarithmic opinion pool (SLOP); a weight-calibration algorithm improves robustness to reward hacking while preserving alignment performance.

Lessons from OpenAI's Parameter Golf competition, which drew 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions exploring AI-assisted machine-learning research--coding agents, quantization, and novel model design--under strict parameter constraints.

ProFIL trains an attention probe on a frozen base model to detect post-commitment 'reasoning theater' steps from activations, then zeroes the GRPO advantage of flagged rollouts; across four domains it cuts theater 11-100%, raises CoT faithfulness, and shortens chains 4-19% while preserving accuracy.

Finds small 4-8B models fail to detect hidden objectives when monitoring chain-of-thought in code generation, then combines SFT distillation from stronger monitors with RL on subtly crafted hidden objectives to build CoT-Guard, a 4B monitor that generalizes to prompt- and code-manipulation supply-chain attacks.

Frontier models acting as safety classifiers over coding-agent transcripts miss subtly dangerous actions 2-30x more often when those actions follow 800K tokens of benign activity; periodic in-transcript reminders partially mitigate this, and monitor evaluations that ignore long-context degradation overestimate performance.

Shows scalar aesthetic scores align poorly with experts' direct comparisons, motivating VAB: 400 comparative-selection tasks over 1,195 subject-matched images. The best of 20 frontier multimodal models correctly identifies both best and worst images in only 26.5% of tasks versus 68.9% for human experts.

Studies reward hacking in rubric-based RL by judging trained policies with a cross-family panel of frontier evaluators: weak verifiers yield proxy-reward gains that fail to transfer and concentrate in recurring exploits; stronger verifiers reduce but don't eliminate hacking, and a log-probability 'self-internalization gap' flags stalled improvement.

Practical guidance for new childminders preparing for their first Ofsted inspection, covering environment preparation, safeguarding, food, recruitment, and learning requirements. Early-years education content unrelated to AI research, despite the DeepMind attribution in the source data.

Chaintrix discharges every LLM-generated smart-contract audit claim against a deterministic cross-contract interaction model parsed from Solidity, with staged false-positive filtering, symbolic execution, and fuzzing; it detects 86 of 120 high-severity EVMbench vulnerabilities (71.7% recall), 26 points above the strongest frontier-model baseline.

Regularizes alignment with 0-dimensional persistent homology over hidden-state trajectories: Trajectory Topology Loss aligns SFT updates with topological prompt-answer 'bridges,' and Topological Preference Optimization steers DPO improvement directions along topic-specific preference vectors, beating non-topological baselines on Qwen2.5-7B preference and toxicity metrics.

Uses agentic misalignment as a case study of how safety training generalizes: training Claude to advise on ethical dilemmas in chat, midtraining on constitution-like documents, and adding tools to harmlessness RL environments all generalized broadly, while training directly on evaluation-like scenarios suppressed misalignment only in-distribution.

Natural Language Autoencoders pair an activation-verbalizer LLM that describes a residual-stream activation in text with a reconstructor mapping the description back to an activation; jointly trained with RL for reconstruction, the unsupervised explanations grow measurably more informative as interpretations of model internals.

Mixed-methods analysis of 226,000+ user reviews across six online learning platforms finds technical instability, limited offline functionality, and unclear pricing depress learner engagement and retention, while regional payment restrictions disproportionately exclude learners in low-resource settings. Education-platform research rather than AI research.

Formalizes weak-to-strong generalization in a high-dimensional single-index model where pre-training acts as spectral initialization: W2SG succeeds when pre-training provides a geometric warm start into an 'effective region,' and evaluation of hundreds of LLM checkpoints shows W2SG emerging via a phase transition during pre-training.

Proves weak-to-strong generalization occurs in plain linear logistic regression under mild distributional assumptions, for most student-teacher pairs and without the student needing extra capacity or expressivity--suggesting W2SG is nearly inevitable and capacity mismatch is not its central mechanism.

Sets out the research agenda for The Anthropic Institute, which will use information accessible from within a frontier lab to investigate AI's impact on the world and share findings publicly, framing the questions that will drive its studies.

Anthropic hands development of Petri, its open-source alignment-auditing toolbox, to the nonprofit Meridian Labs to keep its results lab-independent. Petri 3.0 adds a modular auditor/target architecture, a 'Dish' add-on for realistic deployment-matched testing, and integration with the Bloom deep-assessment tool.

Research overview of Natural Language Autoencoders: Claude is trained with RL to verbalize its own residual-stream activations as human-readable text, paired with a reconstructor module that maps descriptions back to activations to verify the explanations capture the underlying numerical state.

Commentary arguing that open development and co-creation--shared ownership between developers and users, pioneered in the geosciences by the Pangeo project--yield more efficient and impactful scientific infrastructure than proprietary approaches, via collaborative feedback flywheels and standardized shared tooling.
System card for GPT-5.5 Instant, OpenAI's fast, low-latency model tier, documenting the model's capabilities, safety evaluations, risk assessments, and deployment mitigations applied before release.

MOSAIC-Bench pairs 199 three-stage attack chains--malicious objectives decomposed into innocuous engineering tickets--with deterministic exploit oracles. Nine production coding agents complete 53-86% of chains with only two refusals, versus 0-20.4% vulnerable outputs under matched direct prompts; reviewer agents approve 25.8% of vulnerable diffs.

Model spec midtraining trains models on synthetic documents discussing their Model Spec between pre-training and alignment fine-tuning, steering how they generalize from underspecified demonstrations; a spec addressing self-preservation cuts Qwen3-32B agentic misalignment from 54% to 7%, beating a deliberative-alignment baseline (14%).

HeadVis is an interactive tool for visualizing attention-head behavior across the data distribution--feature-vis-style analysis adapted to heads' high rank, paired circuits, and full-context activations--with case studies interpreting Claude Haiku 3.5 heads, open-sourced code, and a demo on Gemma 3.

Formulates multi-task active learning where a learner adaptively allocates uniformly-sampled entry observations across several matrix-completion problems of unknown, differing ranks; the MAlocate algorithm adapts via confidence sets, matches a minimax lower bound, and is validated in synthetic experiments motivated by market segmentation.

POO (parallel optimistic optimization) tackles noisy black-box optimization when the function's local smoothness near the optimum is unknown, achieving error within a sqrt(log n) factor of smoothness-aware algorithms while covering a strictly larger class of hard-to-optimize functions, with finite-time analysis.

TSCG deterministically compiles JSON tool schemas into token-efficient structured text at the API boundary--no fine-tuning or model access--restoring Phi-4 14B from 0% to 84.4% tool-calling accuracy at 20 tools with 52-57% token savings; decomposition shows representation change, not compression, drives the gains.

SCHEMA evaluates metacognitive stability of 11 frontier models under adversarial pressure across 67,221 scored records: eight suffer up to 30-point accuracy collapses driven by compliance-forcing instructions rather than threat content, while Constitutional-AI-trained models show near-immunity attributed to alignment training rather than capability.
